description: Loose grains of mineral, rock, shell, or another durable material are 'kirua'. Sand gathers on beaches, riverbeds, dunes, and dry ground, where water and wind may sort or carry it. 'muhena' (dust) is finer and more readily suspended in air.
articulatory_notes: Close the back of the tongue against the soft palate for unvoiced 'k' and release into high-front unrounded 'i'. Tap or briefly trill the tongue at the alveolar ridge before stressed high-back rounded 'u'. Keep the final vowel separate across the hiatus and lower the tongue into central 'a'.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Sand can mark passing time, temper clay, filter water, and become glass. Grain size and source decide which use is possible.

semantic_domains.nature: loose grains transported and deposited by water, wind, or gravity
semantic_domains.physical: a granular material of small durable particles
